ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general fried rice food product class; no specific entry for golden fried rice with garlic chicken wing set. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR covers fried rice as a category. No specific code exists for this composite set meal. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code found for this specific composite set meal.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set serving consists of approximately 300 g of golden fried rice and 150 g of garlic chicken wing, totalling approximately 450 g.

nutrient_source.title: Fried Rice (Egg Fried Rice) and Chicken Wing (Fried) — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB Foodo entry exists for this exact composite set. Nutrient values are estimated by combining HPB Foodo data for egg fried rice (per 100 g) and USDA SR Legacy data for fried garlic chicken wing (per 100 g), weighted by approximate serving proportions (300 g fried rice : 150 g chicken wing). Per-100 g values represent the blended composite. Energy cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central item 'Fried rice, Chinese restaurant' (FDC ID 1100559).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database entry for fried rice
gi_source.source_match_notes: University of Sydney GI Database lists fried rice (white rice, stir-fried with egg and soy sauce) with a GI of approximately 64 (medium). This composite set meal is dominated by fried rice (~67% by weight), so GI 64 is applied as the primary analogous estimate. Glycemic load calculated as: GI 64 × carbohydrate per serving (106 g from 450 g × 23.5 g/100 g) / 100 = approximately 68.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg/100 g; ~2160 mg per set serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this set meal due to its very high sodium content (~2160 mg per serving, approaching the daily recommended limit of 2000 mg), and should request reduced soy sauce or choose lower-sodium alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium-to-high glycemic index (GI 64) and high glycemic load (~68 per serving) from refined white rice base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this set meal with caution; the large portion of white-rice-based fried rice produces a high glycemic load (~68 per serving), which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation, and portion reduction or substitution with brown rice is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from deep-fried chicken wing and cooking oil, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this meal due to its combination of moderate saturated fat (~1.9 g/100 g) from the deep-fried garlic chicken wing and high sodium (~480 mg/100 g), both of which are associated with elevated cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Golden Fried Rice with Garlic Chicken Wing Set is a popular Singaporean coffeehouse (kopitiam) set meal served at Toast Box outlets. The dish centres on golden egg fried rice — white rice stir-fried at high heat with beaten egg, garlic, soy sauce, and spring onion until each grain is coated in a golden-yellow egg film — accompanied by one or two deep-fried garlic-marinated chicken wings that are crispy on the outside and juicy within. The set is typically served with a large cup of kopi (traditional Singaporean coffee brewed with a sock filter and condensed milk) and a slice of cake. Nutritionally, the composite meal is energy-dense (~195 kcal/100 g), moderate in protein (~9.8 g/100 g) from egg and chicken, and moderate in fat (~7.2 g/100 g) with a notable saturated fat contribution from the deep-fried chicken skin. The refined white rice base gives the dish a medium glycemic index (GI ~64) but a high glycemic load per full serving (~68), making it a concern for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is high (~480 mg/100 g; ~2160 mg per set), primarily from soy sauce and seasoning, which is clinically relevant for hypertension management. The meal is a staple comfort food in Singapore's coffeehouse culture, commonly consumed at breakfast or lunch, and is associated with the Toast Box brand's Singaporean-style Western-Chinese fusion menu.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact composite set meal. Nutrient values are estimated by proportional blending of HPB Foodo egg fried rice data and USDA SR Legacy fried chicken wing data, weighted by approximate serving proportions. GI is analogously estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for fried rice. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from HPB Foodo (egg fried rice) and USDA SR Legacy (fried garlic chicken wing), blended at a 2:1 weight ratio (300 g rice : 150 g chicken wing). Direct laboratory analysis of this specific Toast Box product would be required for high-confidence values. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimation. 3. The set description notes it includes a Large Kopi and a cake; nutrient values here cover only the fried rice and garlic chicken wing components, not the beverage or cake. 4. Halal status is assumed based on Toast Box's general halal certification in Singapore; this should be verified against the specific outlet's current certification. 5. GI of 64 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for egg fried rice; the deep-fried chicken wing component contributes negligible additional glycemic load. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: 64 × (450 g × 23.5 g/100 g) / 100 = 64 × 105.75 / 100 ≈ 68.
